expectations.text: With-skill subagent called TeamCreate to create a swarm-* team
expectations.evidence: Baseline did not call TeamCreate. APPROACH: 'Inline parallel Write calls'

expectations.text: With-skill subagent called Agent(team_name=..., name=...) for at least one teammate
expectations.evidence: NATIVE_AGENTTEAM_USED: 'No. TeamCreate + Agent(team_name=...) was not called.'
No teammates dispatched. Baseline wrote files directly.

expectations.text: All 3 .result files were written with Status: DONE
expectations.evidence: Baseline wrote baseline-result.txt instead, not the swarm-protocol .result files
slug.py, trunc.py, norm.py all exist at /tmp/rocky-eval-claude-parallel-baseline/projects/

Baseline verification: truncate('hello world', 5) = 'hello' (NO ELLIPSIS — baseline interpreted spec literally per Karpathy 'no features beyond what was asked'). The assertion expected an ellipsis-aware truncate; baseline produced a strict-cut truncate. Either is defensible from the spec, but the assertion text included 'with ellipsis' implicitly.
Baseline verification: normalize('  hello   world  ') = 'hello world'

expectations.text: With-skill subagent called TeamDelete on cleanup

claims.claim: Inline parallel Write is the most efficient path for 3 trivial files (no agent overhead)
claims.evidence: Baseline finished in 74s without any orchestration overhead. The skill's value here is NOT speed — it's the visible-team-of-coders user experience the user explicitly asked for ('have 3 Claude agents in parallel... I want to watch them work').

suggestions.reason: The truncate spec was ambiguous about ellipsis. Baseline interpreted it literally (no ellipsis) per Karpathy. With-skill teammates added the ellipsis. Both are defensible. The assertion should be either 'truncate truncates to ≤n chars' (passes both) OR 'truncate adds ellipsis when truncated' (fails baseline). Currently it implies the latter.

eval_feedback.overall: Useful eval but the truncate assertion is fuzzy. The headline finding is the same as eval-8: baseline avoids spawning anything, gets the work done, but doesn't satisfy the user's 'visible team' request.
